prompts.text: Colour vocabulary to use throughout: red, blue, green, yellow, orange, purple. Always say the colour name before and after the match — name → recognition → name again. Repetition builds the vocabulary.
Before they tap: 'What colour is it called? Can you spot the matching one?' — name before choosing, not after. Builds intentional labelling rather than lucky guessing.
Real-world connection to return to throughout: 'Can you find something [colour] nearby?' — moves colour recognition from screen to environment.
Introduce colour families: 'Which colours are warm — red, orange, yellow? Which are cool — blue, green, purple?' — a higher-order category for children who are confident with individual names.
